tips.permaculture: Mulch generously at the base to retain moisture and naturally fertilize.
tips.conventionnel: Water regularly at the base, never on leaves to prevent disease.
tips.naturel: Use mature compost and nettle tea to strengthen plants.
tips.industriel: Apply NPK fertilizer rich in potassium during flowering.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Plant after the last frost date. Protect from late frosts.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Monitor watering during heat waves. Prune suckers.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Harvest before first frost. Make preserves.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Plan rotations. Order seeds.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: Compost in spring, nettle tea every 2 weeks during growth
fertilizerSchedule.industriel: Growth fertilizer at start of season, flowering fertilizer from first flowers

tips.permaculture: Pair with basil which repels aphids.
tips.conventionnel: Requires heat and sun. Water regularly without excess.
tips.naturel: Comfrey tea promotes flowering and fruiting.
tips.industriel: Special pepper fertilizer rich in potassium and phosphorus.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Start indoors in February. Transplant mid-May after frost.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Water more frequently during intense heat.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Harvest before frost.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Store in cool cellar or freeze.

tips.permaculture: Excellent ground cover to smother weeds. Integrate into raised beds.
tips.conventionnel: Water abundantly and regularly to prevent bitterness.
tips.naturel: Abundant compost at base. Nettle tea if yellowing.
tips.industriel: Nitrogen fertilizer at start of growth.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow in pots in April, transplant mid-May.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Harvest young (15-20 cm) for continuous production.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Frost will end production.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Prepare a compost bin for next season.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: Abundant compost at planting, nettle tea monthly
fertilizerSchedule.industriel: Slow-release granular fertilizer at start of season

tips.permaculture: Guide on willow or bamboo trellis to save space.
tips.conventionnel: Water daily. Guide on trellis.
tips.naturel: Nettle tea every 2 weeks for vigor.
tips.industriel: Balanced fertilizer then potassium-rich at flowering.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow under cover in April.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Prune aerial parts after 6-8 leaves. Harvest often.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Final harvests before frost.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Disinfect greenhouse if used.

tips.permaculture: Perfect under tomatoes to benefit from summer shade.
tips.conventionnel: Water in morning to prevent leaf diseases.
tips.naturel: Compost tea promotes rapid growth.
tips.industriel: Nitrogen fertilizer for nice full heads.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow from March under cover, then outdoors from April.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Choose heat-resistant varieties. Water early morning.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Good season for lettuce. Protect with row cover end of season.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Grow under tunnels or in greenhouse.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: Light compost at planting, that's enough

tips.permaculture: Sow with onion to repel carrot rust fly.
tips.conventionnel: Deep loose soil, no stones. Avoid fresh manure.
tips.naturel: Carrot needs no fertilizer. Light soil enough.
tips.industriel: Avoid nitrogen fertilizers. Favor potassium and phosphorus.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow from March directly in place. Mix seeds with sand.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Water deeply but rarely.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Harvest before hard frost or mulch to store in ground.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Store in sand in cool place.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: No fertilizer, soil prepared with mature compost previous fall
fertilizerSchedule.industriel: Potassium fertilizer only if soil very poor

tips.permaculture: Sow at edges or between crops to mark rows.
tips.conventionnel: Fast growth. Harvest young to avoid hollowing and bitterness.
tips.naturel: Almost self-sufficient. Light compost layer enough.
tips.industriel: Minimal fertilizer, excess nitrogen gives leaves not roots.

seasonalAdvice.spring: First crop from March. Ideal as intercrop.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Avoid intense heat periods (rapid bolting).
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Excellent fall crop. Good flavor in cool weather.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Winter radishes (Daikon type) resist cold.

tips.permaculture: Nitrogen fixer. Plant with corn and zucchini (the three sisters).
tips.conventionnel: Don't water too much. Beans dislike wet feet.
tips.naturel: Rhizobium inoculant to maximize atmospheric nitrogen fixation.
tips.industriel: Little fertilizer needed thanks to natural nitrogen fixation.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow after last frost (late May). Soil must be warm.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Harvest young and often to extend production.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Let some pods mature for seeds.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Store dry seeds for next year.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: Rhizobium inoculant at planting, no extra fertilizer

tips.permaculture: Nitrogen-fixing legume. Ideal winter/spring crop.
tips.conventionnel: Tolerate cold well. Water moderately.
tips.naturel: Nettle tea if leaves yellow.
tips.industriel: Little nitrogen fertilizer due to natural fixation.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow from March-April directly in place.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Heat ends production. Harvest before.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Fall sowing for spring harvest.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Plants resist light frost.

tips.permaculture: Excellent tomato companion. Repels pests.
tips.conventionnel: Pinch off flowers to extend leaf production.
tips.naturel: Garlic infusion against aphids. No strong fertilizer.
tips.industriel: Light foliar fertilizer if leaf yellowing.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow indoors from April. Transplant after frost.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Good production. Pinch flowers regularly.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Bring plants indoors before frost.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Indoor culture possible on sunny windowsill.

tips.permaculture: Excellent garden border. Attracts beneficial insects.
tips.conventionnel: Slow germination. Soak seeds 24h before sowing.
tips.naturel: Undemanding. Small amount of compost sufficient.
tips.industriel: Light nitrogen fertilizer for nice green color.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow from March. Soak seeds 24h before to speed germination.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Cut regularly to stimulate regrowth.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Resists light frost. Continue harvesting.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Protect with row cover.

tips.permaculture: Plant in buried pot as very invasive. Repels pests.
tips.conventionnel: Contain in pot or border. Water regularly.
tips.naturel: Very hardy. Almost no fertilizer needed.
tips.industriel: Minimal fertilizer, excess nitrogen reduces intensity.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Divide clumps to multiply. Ideal period.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Water to prevent drying.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Cut back to 10 cm. Plant regrows in spring.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Dormancy. Protect in pot in cold zones.

tips.permaculture: Mulch with straw to protect fruit and retain moisture.
tips.conventionnel: Drip irrigate to prevent disease.
tips.naturel: Leaf compost. Comfrey tea during flowering.
tips.industriel: Special strawberry fertilizer rich in potassium.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Plant new plants. Remove flowers first year.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Harvest regularly. Remove runners except for propagation.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Clean old leaves. Mulch for winter.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Protect in severe cold.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: Compost at planting, comfrey tea monthly during flowering
fertilizerSchedule.industriel: Granular strawberry fertilizer spring and fall

tips.permaculture: Culture possible under thick mulch without digging (straw culture).
tips.conventionnel: Hill regularly to increase production.
tips.naturel: Horsetail decoction prevents late blight.
tips.industriel: Potato-specific fertilizer rich in potassium.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Plant sprouted seed potatoes March-April per climate.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Water abundantly. Hill one last time. Monitor blight.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Harvest in dry weather. Store in cool dark cellar.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Prepare seed potatoes by setting to sprout in January.

tips.permaculture: Excellent carrot companion. Repels carrot rust fly.
tips.conventionnel: Reduce watering late in season to promote drying.
tips.naturel: Undemanding. 3-4 year rotation required.
tips.industriel: Potassium-rich fertilizer for nice bulbs.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Plant sets from March when soil warms.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Let tops fall over, sign of maturity.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Harvest and dry indoors.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Store braided in dry airy place.

tips.permaculture: Excellent natural repellent. Plant at garden border.
tips.conventionnel: Plant cloves point up. Little water needed.
tips.naturel: Well-drained soil. Few inputs needed.
tips.industriel: Avoid nitrogen fertilizers that promote disease.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Fall-planted garlic in full growth. Stop watering.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Harvest when tops yellow and fall over.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Ideal planting October-November for July harvest.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Garlic resists frost well. Little maintenance.

tips.permaculture: Pair with carrots to repel each other's pests.
tips.conventionnel: Transplant 15-20 cm deep for nice white shanks.
tips.naturel: Compost at planting. Regular watering.
tips.industriel: Nitrogen fertilizer to promote shank development.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow March-April. Transplant June.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Regular watering in heat.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Begin harvests. Cold improves flavor.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Frost resistant. Harvest as needed until spring.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: Compost at transplant, nettle tea monthly
fertilizerSchedule.industriel: Nitrogen fertilizer at transplant and mid-growth

tips.permaculture: Short-season crop ideal for filling gaps.
tips.conventionnel: Spring and fall crop. Avoid heat.
tips.naturel: Compost tea for intense green color.
tips.industriel: Nitrogen fertilizer for well-developed leaves.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow from March. Rapid harvest before heat.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Avoid, bolts quickly in heat.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Excellent fall crop until frost.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Row cover to extend harvest.

tips.permaculture: Very productive. Let some plants bolt for self-seeding.
tips.conventionnel: Water regularly. Harvest leaf by leaf.
tips.naturel: Abundant compost. Very disease resistant.
tips.industriel: Nitrogen fertilizer for big beautiful leaves.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow in place from April.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Abundant production. Harvest regularly.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Resists early frost well.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Row cover to extend harvest.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: Compost at planting, nettle tea monthly

tips.permaculture: Tops are edible. Let some plants bolt for self-seeding.
tips.conventionnel: Thin to 10 cm for nice round roots.
tips.naturel: Mature compost at planting. Undemanding.
tips.industriel: Avoid nitrogen fertilizers favoring tops over roots.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Direct sow from April. Deep loose soil.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Water regularly to prevent splitting.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Harvest before hard frost. Store in sand.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Store in cool cellar in moist sand.

tips.permaculture: Flowers attract pollinators. Pair with borage.
tips.conventionnel: Harvest central head before flowers open. Side heads regrow.
tips.naturel: Nettle tea every 3 weeks. Avoid excess nitrogen.
tips.industriel: Nitrogen at transplant, then phosphorus and potassium at head formation.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow March under cover. Transplant May.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Water abundantly. Watch for caterpillars.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Excellent fall crop. Cold improves flavor.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Protect with row cover in hard frost.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: Rich compost at transplant, nettle tea monthly
fertilizerSchedule.industriel: Brassica fertilizer at transplant and mid-growth

tips.permaculture: Pair with beans and zucchini (three sisters). Corn supports beans.
tips.conventionnel: Plant in square blocks (min 4x4) for good wind pollination.
tips.naturel: Nettle tea and abundant compost. Hungry plant.
tips.industriel: Nitrogen early growth, potassium before flowering.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Sow under cover in April, transplant mid-May when warm (>12°C).
seasonalAdvice.summer: Water abundantly especially during flowering and ear formation.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Harvest when kernels fully formed and silks brown.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Chop stalks for compost or mulch.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: Very abundant compost at planting, nettle tea every 2 weeks
fertilizerSchedule.industriel: Nitrogen (30-0-0) during growth, complete fertilizer at flowering

tips.permaculture: Attracts pollinators and birds. Excellent natural windbreak.
tips.conventionnel: Face sun. Stake tall varieties against wind.
tips.naturel: Very hardy. Little compost enough. Avoid excess nitrogen.
tips.industriel: Potassium and phosphate for nice heads, avoid nitrogen.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Direct sow May when frost danger passed. Sunny soil.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Water regularly but not excessive. Plant tolerates drought.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Harvest heads when seeds detach easily. Dry before storage.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Leave some heads for birds or harvest seeds for next year.

fertilizerSchedule.naturel: Light compost at planting, no extra fertilizer
fertilizerSchedule.industriel: Phospho-potassium fertilizer only at early flowering

tips.permaculture: Observe plant's natural needs and integrate into your ecosystem.
tips.conventionnel: Adjust watering to your plant's specific needs.
tips.naturel: Compost and nettle tea suit most plants.
tips.industriel: Balanced universal fertilizer per manufacturer instructions.

seasonalAdvice.spring: Ideal time to plant most vegetation.
seasonalAdvice.summer: Increase watering in hot dry weather.
seasonalAdvice.autumn: Prepare plant for winter.
seasonalAdvice.winter: Protect from frost if needed.
